author: Marco
msg:
I think people have connected ~100, but no one will guarantee it’s most efficient for rewards or for the real world utility of the network. They want diversity to be an organic characteristic of the edge network Unity is creating, so clustered devices aren’t encouraged. Obviously there’s always gray area when considering what’s best for you and what’s best for the project you are a part of.

Just a week or two ago if you exceeded 20 devices your rewards dropped sharply. Today it seems you can get $0.20-$0.30 per device up to 40 or more. Tomorrow? Who knows.

msg:
[File: IMG_1017.MP4] 🤝 **Unity Network - Rewards Update**

During routine network monitoring, we identified operators running thousands of synthetic devices through VPNs, emulators, and /24 IP ranges. These devices were affecting the commercial value of the network. Those accounts have now been addressed, and the network is recalibrating. Please bear with us during this process as reward distribution normalises for genuine operators.

**Why This Matters**
Synthetic activity undermines the commercial value of the entire network. When partners and customers detect synthetic devices, it erodes their confidence in the data and the network itself. Protecting network integrity protects your earning potential.

Please note, there is no limit to the number of devices you may run from a single IP range; however, if a partner or customer determines that your devices are synthetic rather than real-world, it may affect or limit your ability to receive tasks. To maximise reward potential, we recommend running no more than approximately 10 to 20 devices per network subnet, using real telephones, whether headless (for example, those without screens) or fully functional (with screens).

**What's Ahead**
As the network recalibrates, tasks will begin flowing back to legitimate ULOs rather than being consumed by synthetic operators. Your existing telemetry tasks will continue to run as normal. On top of this, we're preparing several new task types for release in the near term, including Scout & Runner (S&R) alongside additional WiFi and SIM powered tasks. Each new task type expands overall reward potential across the network.

Unity Network remains dedicated to delivering a best-in-class edge network experience through the application, with web-based management platforms on the way. There are multiple exciting releases scheduled for March, April, and beyond that have not yet been communicated.

We're also excited to announce that we'll be hosting an AMA with the founder of one of the new third-party projects - a partnership we believe will redefine what rewards mean for ULO operators.

**Questions?**
Submit them to the official channel using #AMA. Your questions will be answered at the UNO meeting on 7 April 2026. Details are available via the X post: https://x.com/UnityNodesIO/status/2036554063936909778

Your feedback matters. We're always working to improve the operator experience and reward structure.

UNOs and ULOs, thank you for your continued support whilst we build out the largest smartphone-driven, multi-versatile, composable edge network.

author: EdgyBruh
msg:
With the new IP/subnet filtering, it's no longer enough to just run devices on different ISPs. If the public IP or subnet is the same, those devices are still grouped as a cluster and rewards will be shit.
From what I've seen, the key factor now is having unique public IPs, not just different providers. No more than 5 -10 devices per unique public IP to stay within safe limits.
So optimization now (from what I can see in my setup) is less about adding more SIMs or ISPs, and more about managing IP distribution and density per IP

author: Mr Roundry
msg:
Nothing has changed in terms of boundaries or recommended devices - up to 20 per location, same as always. 

What changed is that thousands of ULOs were abusing the system - VPNs, synthetic networking, emulators, synthetic devices and more.

That abuse was damaging the network’s value and degrading the quality of data being delivered to telemetry customers. 

Lower quality can mean lower demand - and demand is what drives your rewards. The cleanup removed those bad actors, but the network is still recalibrating after that. Give it time.​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​

author: capri
msg:
Let’s be honest and speak openly. Otherwise, we’re not only misleading ourselves, but also everyone around us.

They strongly pushed and incentivized us to buy nodes — encouraging many of us to go for at least 3 nodes, meaning a minimum of 600 licenses. We trusted them and invested.

Now the real question is: what are we supposed to do with these licenses? Keep them as souvenirs to remind us how naive we were — or try to lease them under conditions that simply don’t make sense?

Who is realistically going to lease them, running devices 24/7 with SIM cards, draining battery, consuming internet — while earning rewards that don’t even cover the operating costs? And even if they barely do, convincing people to participate under these conditions is extremely difficult.

We tried to be proactive. As a “solution,” many of us invested even more in farming setups to support the project and help it succeed — hoping we could at least cover our costs. But instead of mutual support to sustain the ecosystem, the rewards were reduced, making it impossible to cover even basic operating expenses.

We bought nodes (licenses), devices, SIM cards, internet subscriptions, routers, and networking equipment — and we invested our valuable time.

This is not just frustrating — it feels deeply unfair, misleading, and completely unsustainable.
